preamble: The shift from a high front nasalized vowel [ĩ] to a high back nasalized vowel [ũ] involves rounding and backing, often triggered by coarticulation with labial or velar consonants.

examples.note: Rounding of high nasalized vowels, often in proximity to labialized segments.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
